content.problemStatement:
Chef is judging a game called "Broken telephone". There are total **N** players taking part in the game. They are all sitting in a line. In the start of the game, first player is given a secret message written on a sheet of paper. Then they keep sending the message by whispering it to the player sitting immediate right to one and so on until it reaches the last person. 

Finally, the message received by the last player is compared with the message said by first player. If these messages aren't equal, there is someone who has misheard the message or whispered it wrongly to the next player. If messages is equal, then the players win and receive a tasty chocolate. 

Note that first player receives the message on a sheet of paper, thus he cannot mishear it.

As Chef wants to be sure that every player has fulfilled his/ her role in the game, so he asks everyone to state their received messages after the end of the game. You are given an array **A** of **N** integers denoting messages received by each person.

Please help Chef to find the number of players that could mishear the message or whisper it wrongly.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two lines. The first line contains a single integer N, the number of players. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, ..., AN denoting the messages received by each player.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single integer corresponding to the number of players that could mishear the message or whisper it wrongly.

sampleTestCases.explanation: In the first test case, the 3rd, 4th, 6th, and 7th players could mishear the message or whisper it wrongly. In the second test case, the first 3 players could mishear the message or whisper it wrongly. In the third test case, no player could mishear the message or whisper it wrongly.
In this test case, all players received the same message, so no player could mishear the message or whisper it wrongly.
In this test case, all players except the first one could mishear the message or whisper it wrongly.

solution.approach: The key insight is to compare each player's message with the first player's message. If they are different, it means the message was misheard or whispered wrongly. We iterate through the array of messages and count the number of players that could mishear the message or whisper it wrongly.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T*N) total.
